'''Umyla Hanley''' (born 5 March 2002) is a professional [[rugby league]] [[footballer]] who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Leigh Leopards]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Hanley played his amateur rugby league for the Shevington Sharks and is the son of former [[Bradford Northern|Bradford]], [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], [[Western Suburbs Magpies|Western Suburbs]], [[Balmain Tigers|Balmain]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] international, [[Ellery Hanley]].<ref name="The Guardian - 8 January 2020 - Wigan kick off the new Super League season buoyed by young talent">{{cite news |last1=Willacy |first1=Gavin |title=Wigan kick off the new Super League season buoyed by young talent |url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/no-helmets-required/2020/jan/27/wigan-warriors-super-league-season-young-blood |access-date=4 October 2020 |work=[[The Guardian]] |date=28 January 2020}}</ref>

==Career==
===2020===
Hanley made his Super League debut in round 14 of the [[Super League XXV results|2020 Super League season]] for the Warriors against [[St Helens RLFC|St Helens]] where Wigan went on to lose 42–0 against a much more experienced St Helens team, Hanley started at fullback and became Wigan Warrior player #1105.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1587/round-14-fixtures-confirmed|title=Round 14 fixtures confirmed |publisher=Sky Sports |date=22 September 2020}}</ref>
<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/wigan-vs-st-helens/teams/54569 |title=Starting Lineups - Wigan vs St Helens |publisher=Sky Sports |date=29 September 2020}}</ref>
<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/54330327 |title=Super League: Wigan vs St Helens |publisher=BBC |date=29 September 2020}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/debut-a-moment-ill-never-forget-says-kai-pearce-paul/ |title=Debut a moment "I'll never forget" says Kai Pearce Paul |publisher=Love Rugby League |date=29 September 2020}}</ref>

===2021===
In round 17 of the [[2021 Super League season]], Hanley scored a [[hat-trick]] in Wigan's 50-6 victory over Leigh.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58049907|title=Wigan Warriors thump winless Leigh Centurions 50-6|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===2022===
Hanley made only one appearance for Wigan in the 2022 Super League season. He spent the majority of the year on loan with [[RFL Championship]] side [[Newcastle Thunder|Newcastle]] playing ten games.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.wigantoday.net/sport/rugby-league/wigan-warriors-here-is-how-the-loan-and-dual-registration-players-have-performed-in-june-3746396/|title=Wigan Warriors: Here is how the loan and dual-registration players have performed in June|website=www.wigantoday.net}}</ref>

===2024===
In round 6 of the 2024 Super League season, Hanley scored a [[hat-trick]] in Leigh's 32-22 loss against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/powerhouse-umyla-hanley-impresses-leigh-leopards-boss-in-prolific-try-scoring-stint/|title='Powerhouse' Umyla Hanley impresses Leigh Leopards boss in prolific try-scoring stint|website=www.totalrl.com}}</ref>

===2025===
In round 3 of the 2025 Super League season, Hanley scored a [[hat-trick]] in Leigh's 34-6 home win against [[Catalans Dragons|Catalan Dragons]]. Hanley played 24 games for Leigh in the 2025 Super League season including the clubs semi-final loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/3-key-areas-that-saw-leigh-leopards-miss-out-on-old-trafford-as-grand-final-opportunity-passed-up/|title=3 key areas that saw Leigh Leopards miss out on Old Trafford as Grand Final opportunity passed up|website=www.loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>
